- Removed zoom buttons for cleaner interface
- Added helpful hint text for mouse controls

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add CWE v4.19.1 per-domain security audit taxonomies (#230)

Ingest the MITRE CWE v4.19.1 corpus (969 weaknesses, 944 active) and
generate 13 per-domain taxonomy files for PromptKit security audits.

Artifacts:
- scripts/ingest-cwe.py: standalone, reusable ingestion script
- data/cwe/4.19.1/: normalized data, domain mappings, metadata
- taxonomies/cwe-*.md: 13 domain taxonomy files (626 mapped CWEs)

Domains: kernel-mode-c-cpp (113), native-user-mode-c-cpp (172),
managed-dotnet (92), web-js-ts (265), web-backend (423),
cloud-service (113), iac (8), firmware-embedded (226),
crypto-protocols (63), data-processing (69), cli-tools (65),
mobile-app (23), container-k8s (5).

Mapping uses 4-priority algorithm: CWE view membership, platform
match, context/keyword analysis, and override rules. All domain
exclusion and consistency checks pass.

Template integration (domain parameter support) tracked in Issue #228.

Co-authored-by: Alan Jowett <alan.jowett@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: PromptKit
repository.description: Agentic prompts are the most important code you're not engineering. PromptKit fixes that — composable, version-controlled prompt components (personas, protocols, formats, templates) that snap together into reliable, repeatable prompts for bug investigation, design docs, code review, security audits, and more. Works with any LLM.

commit.message:
Strengthen reviewer skill: add step-back analysis dimensions (#13504)

Learnings from reviewing PR #13477 — the 24-dimension checklist missed
design-level issues that required stepping back from the diff.

### Changes
- **Dim 4 (Tests)**: Flag weak assertions that would pass with incorrect
output
- **Dim 10 (Design)**: Check alignment with original design intent, flag
workarounds when better APIs exist in existing dependencies, validate
borrowed patterns
- **Dim 22 (Correctness)**: Generalize 2-participant fixes to N
participants, flag symptom-only fixes
- **Workflow**: Add historical context step (read linked issue +
original feature PR) before dispatching dimension agents
- **Tasks instructions**: Document \Microsoft.IO.Redist\ API
availability for .NET Framework

### Why
The review agents evaluated the diff mechanically but missed:
1. The fix patches a TOCTOU symptom; a better API
(\Microsoft.IO.File.Move(overwrite: true)\) exists in an
already-referenced package
2. The fix handles 2 concurrent writers but fails with 3+
3. Test assertions were too weak to verify correct behavior
4. The approach was borrowed from the VS editor (which never has 'file
doesn't exist' case) without validating assumptions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

while section/team cards used YTD stats, causing confusing discrepancies

commit.message:
fix: resolve 4 critical web experience bugs

Bug 1 — Health check false positive: healthCheck() now requires 2xx
or 405 (Method Not Allowed). A 404 from an undeployed Azure Function
no longer fools the app into API mode.

Bug 2 — Track card/pill clicks now send prompts: clicking a track card
or framework pill sets pendingQuickPrompt so the user message auto-sends
after transitioning to chat. No more blank chat on entry.

Bug 3 — Markdown rendering in chat bubbles: added renderMarkdown()
supporting bold, italic, inline code, fenced code blocks, lists,
links, paragraphs, and line breaks. Assistant text messages and
streaming bubbles now render markdown instead of plain text.

Bug 4 — Sessions sidebar UX: added close button, active-session
indicator dot, full-width New session button in footer, styled to
match Azure Portal left-nav conventions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add strict (0,1) validators to RewardBreakdown and RewardPenalties

All float fields in models now have validators ensuring values are
strictly between 0 and 1:
- RewardBreakdown: correctness, reasoning_quality, progress
- RewardPenalties: step_penalty, invalid_action_penalty, repetition_penalty, skip_penalty
- Reward: score (already had validator)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

consensus: fix same-height fork trap — ancestor+1 tiebreaker and immediate finality lock override

Two related fixes for the minority-fork restart attack observed on LW-Michigan:

1. Fork-point hash tiebreaker (fork_resolver.rs)
   Previously the tiebreaker compared the TIP hashes of both chains, which are
   hashes of blocks produced at different times after the fork diverged. Now we
   compare the hashes at common_ancestor+1 — the first block each side produced
   after the split. This is more meaningful (both blocks were produced from the
   same parent at the same slot) and harder to game (the tip hash can be
   influenced by blocks produced much later).
   Falls back to tip-hash comparison when ancestor+1 hashes are not available.

2. Same-height finality lock override (blockchain.rs)
   The finality lock escape required total_ahead > 0, which was permanently 0
   in a same-height fork (our height == consensus height — no peer can be
   'strictly ahead' of us). This caused handle_fork() to block forever even
   with 9/11 peers on the consensus chain, triggering de-registration and the
   watchdog restart loop (AV new compound vector).
   Fix adds MIN_SAME_HEIGHT_CONSENSUS_PEERS=3 immediate bypass: when the fork
   is same-height and >=3 peers independently confirm the consensus hash, lower
   the finality lock to common_ancestor and proceed with the reorg immediately,
   without waiting for the 5-minute escape hatch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use context.attach/detach instead of baggage.attach/detach in auth middleware (#217)

The opentelemetry.baggage module has no attach/detach methods.
These belong to opentelemetry.context. baggage.set_baggage() returns
a Context object that must be activated via context.attach(ctx).

commit.message:
Fix test bugs identified in code review

- Replace broken window.evaluate test with static file analysis
  The Playwright test was trying to evaluate window.createLoungeRoom
  in a non-existent browser context. Changed to match the pattern
  used by other tests: static regex check of lounge-social.js for
  MAX_LOUNGE_OCCUPANTS constant.

- Fix fragile seat-counting regex in unit test
  The regex was matching any object with x and type fields anywhere
  in the file. Now properly scoped to the seats array like the
  Playwright version does.

- Clarify test assertion messages
  Updated comments to indicate tests check 'sufficient seats for
  maxOccupants=5' rather than implying new seats were added.

loading via EAS Update. Firebase config is not a secret (protected by
Firestore rules) so hardcoding as fallback is safe and standard practice
for React Native web Firebase config.

commit.message:
fix: guard module-level Firebase init and Notifications handler

Both ran at import time with no error handling. If Firebase config is
missing (Constants.expoConfig.extra empty in Expo Go EAS Update context)
or expo-notifications fails, the entire app crashed silently before
any React component could mount - hence blank screen with no error shown.

- Firebase.js: wrap initializeApp in try/catch, provide stub fallback
- notificationService.js: wrap setNotificationHandler in try/catch
- App.js: wrap loading state in ErrorBoundary too

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove legacy approval middleware

Keep the session-driven ChatSession flow as the only supported approval path, drop the old coordinator/chat-client surfaces, and keep coverage focused on session-based approvals.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix 403 error tracking + sequential full analysis

- Fix broken error propagation: HTTPError(403) was swallowed by fetch
  methods' except-Exception handlers, preventing _timed_fetch from
  tracking failures. Now fetch methods re-raise HTTPError so _timed_fetch
  properly sets tv_403, data_error, and error telemetry.
- Fix _with_retry to re-raise HTTPError(403) instead of retrying it
  (since _handle_403 already exhausted its own retries).
- Add POST /api/trigger-all endpoint for sequential agent execution
  with progress tracking via GET /api/trigger-all/status.
- Update frontend to use trigger-all with polling progress display.
- Update tests to verify tv_403, tv_403_resources, and fetch stats
  error flags are properly set after 403 exhaustion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(zigbee): Allow ZigbeeEP implementers to handle raw ZCL commands (#12479)

* feat(zigbee): Add raw command handler for ZigbeeEP

Register a raw ZCL command handler via esp_zb_raw_command_handler_register()
that forwards all incoming ZCL commands to endpoint implementations before
the ZBOSS stack processes them.

This allows ZigbeeEP subclasses to observe and optionally consume cluster-
specific commands (e.g., On/Off, Level Control, Off with effect) that were
previously handled only internally by the stack. By default, the handler
returns false so the stack continues normal processing — existing behavior
is fully preserved.

New API:
- zb_raw_command_t struct with cluster_id, command_id, data, etc.
- ZigbeeEP::zbRawCommand() virtual method for subclass override
- ZigbeeEP::onRawCommand() callback registration for user sketches

Closes https://github.com/espressif/arduino-esp32/issues/12025

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(zigbee): Include zboss_api.h in ZigbeeHandlers for raw command handler

The raw command handler uses ZBOSS internal APIs (zb_zcl_parsed_hdr_t,
ZB_BUF_GET_PARAM, zb_buf_begin, etc.) but ZigbeeHandlers.cpp is compiled
as a separate translation unit by Arduino's build system, so it needs
zboss_api.h included directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(zigbee): Replace raw command handler with privilege + custom cluster commands

Replace ZBOSS-internal raw command handler with two ESP-IDF public API
mechanisms for intercepting ZCL commands:

- Privilege commands (ESP_ZB_CORE_CMD_PRIVILEGE_COMMAND_REQ_CB_ID) for
  intercepting standard cluster commands via addPrivilegeCommand()
- Custom cluster commands (ESP_ZB_CORE_CMD_CUSTOM_CLUSTER_REQ_CB_ID)
  for receiving custom cluster commands automatically

This removes the zboss_api.h dependency and custom zb_raw_command_t
struct, replacing them with ESP-IDF public structs
(esp_zb_zcl_privilege_command_message_t and
esp_zb_zcl_custom_cluster_command_message_t) following the same handler
pattern used by all other ZigbeeEP callbacks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(zigbee): Add Zigbee_Privilege_Command example

Demonstrates how to use addPrivilegeCommand() and onPrivilegeCommand()
to intercept standard ZCL cluster commands (On/Off) before the stack
processes them. Works with the existing Zigbee_On_Off_Switch example
or any Zigbee controller as the sender.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(zigbee): Clarify privilege command delegation in example

Only register "Off with effect" (0x40) as a privilege command to
demonstrate that the stack delegates handling to the application.
Standard On/Off/Toggle commands are left to the stack.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(zigbee): Update readme and brief in sketch

* ci(pre-commit): Apply automatic fixes

---------

commit.message:
feat(nhentai): ship v2.3.6 NHentai overhaul (#52)

* feat(nhentai): ship v2.3.6 NHentai overhaul

Rebuild NHentai source to the recovered v2.3.6 state with improved UX, search, and stability.

Highlights:

- Upgrade source metadata/capabilities and add Cloudflare bypass providing support.

- Add endpoint-aware burst rate limiting and safer retry behavior for API usage.

- Expand search/filter pipeline with persisted length, favorites, date, and tag filters.

- Improve popular tag loading/caching and multiselect filter handling for Paperback.

- Refine discover/search pagination flow, sort handling, and related ordering behavior.

- Add richer settings UI controls for display, read-state options, and filter tuning.

- Add NHentai statistics/screen-time tracking form and interceptor data accounting.

- Restore NHentai asset bundle updates (including icon) and settings state helpers.

* fix(nhentai): address PR #52 review comments

- Fix timezone issue in formatDate(): new Date('YYYY-MM-DD') parses as UTC,
  causing day shift in US timezones. Add parseStoredDate() helper that uses
  parseLocalDateKey() for date-only strings to ensure local time parsing.

- Fix removeSpaces conditional branches: all branches were identical, making
  the setting ineffective. Now varies examples between 'EN|' and 'EN | '.

- Add FAVORITES_THRESHOLD_MAX_STATE_KEY reset to resetNHentaiSettings() so
  max favorites threshold is cleared on settings reset.

- Add legacy DATE_MIN/MAX_DAYS_STATE_KEY migration to getDaysOldFilterSetting()
  so existing users' filter values are preserved after upgrade.

- Fix ensureInstallDate() to check for undefined instead of 0, so deliberate
  stats reset to 0 doesn't re-seed totalRead from legacy caches.

- Add STATS_MARK_READ_ON_DESC_COUNT_KEY to resetAllStatistics() and use 0
  for numeric counters to prevent re-seeding after reset.

- Fix removeSpecificTags() to normalize tag prefixes (female:, male:, etc.)
  before matching, so UI can delete tags regardless of stored prefix format.

- Add Cloudflare cookie expiry validation in saveCloudflareBypassCookies()
  to skip storing expired cookies, matching other sources' behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Kakarot Bot <devnull@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove standalone Event Hubs and ADX — use Fabric Eventhouse + Eventstreams

Fabric F8 capacity provides:
- Eventhouse with KQL database (replaces standalone Azure Data Explorer)
- Eventstreams with custom app source (replaces standalone Event Hubs)

This eliminates ~$850/mo in redundant Azure services.

- Delete event-hubs.bicep and data-explorer.bicep modules
- Update main.bicep, deploy.sh, init-kql-tables.sh
- Delete live ADX cluster and Event Hubs namespace

commit.message:
Replace string-typed mouse button/action in InputMouse with enums

Introduce MouseButton and MouseAction enums in EditorAbstraction to
replace raw string parameters in the InputMouse API. This provides
compile-time safety and IntelliSense support for mouse button and
action values throughout the codebase.

- Add MouseButton enum (Left, Middle, Right, Wheel, Move)
- Add MouseAction enum (Press, Release, Drag, Move, ScrollUp/Down/Left/Right)
- Update IEditorClient.InputMouse signature to use enums
- Update all callers: EditorInputHandler, EditorControl, NeovimClient,
  VimClient, and test helpers
- Add ToNeovimString conversion helpers in Global.cs for RPC wire format
- Convert VimClient.EncodeSgrMouse to switch on enum values
- Use 'using MouseButton = AeroVim.Editor.MouseButton' alias in files
  that also import Avalonia.Input to resolve ambiguity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(tflint): remove unused agent_ip variable

KV network_acls was changed to default_action=Allow (RBAC-only model),
making the agent_ip variable unused. TFLint exits 1 on unused variable
warnings, which killed the smoke test via set -eo pipefail.

Removed agent_ip from: variables.tf, azure-pipelines.yml (incl. the
curl/ipify step), and simulate-pipeline.sh.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): remove broken 'npm install -g npm@latest' step

Node 22 already ships with a modern npm. The global upgrade
breaks on the updated runner due to a missing promise-retry
module in the bundled npm.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: split seo-blog.ts to eliminate 725KB chunk warning

Split seo-blog.ts (12580 lines, 410 entries) into two halves:
- seo-blog.ts: ~205 entries (360KB chunk)
- seo-blog-4.ts: ~205 entries (347KB chunk)

Both well under the 500KB Vite warning threshold.
Updated seoService.ts, staticPagesPlugin.ts to load the new chunk.

commit.message:
fix(step3): add doc_coverage_map and doc_context placeholders to prompt template

The step3_script_refs_prompt task_template was missing {doc_coverage_map}
and {doc_context} placeholders. These values were being built in JS and
passed to buildYamlStepPrompt() but silently dropped, leaving the AI with
only script names and issue counts — no actual documentation content to
validate coverage against (prompt alignment score: 6/10).

Changes:
- Add {doc_coverage_map} after Phase 1 Automated Findings section
- Add {doc_context} (renamed to 'Documentation Excerpts (partial)') before Tasks
- Reorder sections for logical flow: findings → coverage → scripts → excerpts
- Parenthetical note on {doc_context} heading signals partial/truncated data
  so the model does not treat absent excerpts as proof of missing docs
- Update changelog comment for step3_script_refs_prompt

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
